data.title: Duke Energy in Indiana prepares for severe storms, possible outages this weekend due to strong thunderstorms and damaging winds.
data.description: In Plainfield, Indiana, Duke Energy is closely monitoring upcoming weather forecasts predicting severe conditions such as strong thunderstorms and damaging winds. The company is gearing up for possible widespread power outages in its service territory this weekend. Being proactive in their approach, Duke Energy is preparing to address the challenges posed by the anticipated weather conditions to ensure minimal disruption for its customers.

data.title: Bitcoin remains below $118,000 as Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin see gains. Key prices: BTC $117,506.77, ETH $3,583.13, DOGE $0.2352. Notable stats: Large transaction volume up by 13.4%, transactions >$100,000 increased from 11,451 to 12,641 in a day, 190,589 traders liquidated in 24 hours for $909.36 million. Notable developments include Litecoin joining Bitcoin as treasury asset and Bit Digital expanding Ethereum treasury beyond 120,000 coins. [Character count: 405]
data.description: The cryptocurrency market is currently experiencing a period of consolidation, with Bitcoin trading below $118,000. The latest prices are as follows: Bitcoin at $117,506.77, Ethereum at $3,583.13, Solana at $178.23, XRP at $3.45, Dogecoin at $0.2352, and Shiba Inu at $0.00001477. Notable statistics reveal a 13.4% increase in large transaction volume and a 12.4% rise in daily active addresses. Transactions exceeding $100,000 have surged from 11,451 to 12,641 in a single day, while exchanges netflows have dropped by 77.7%. Coinglass data indicates that 190,589 traders were liquidated in the last 24 hours, resulting in losses totaling $909.36 million. Among the top gainers and losers in the past day are Pump.fun, SPX6900, and Fartcoin. In other developments, Litecoin has joined Bitcoin as a treasury asset, MEI Pharma has allocated $67 million for Ethereum, and Bit Digital expanded its Ethereum treasury to over 120,000 coins. Additionally, discussions speculate on a $1.1 billion investment in Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs. For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.
